name: foremost-ng
summary:
`foremost-ng` is a Linux-based file recovery tool that extracts files from disk images or devices by scanning for configurable file headers and footers. It supports common forensic formats (e.g., `.dd`, Safeback, Encase) and can operate on both image files and live devices.
> ⚡ This fork aims to modernize the codebase, improve terminal output with ANSI styling, and introduce new features for forensic analysis and data recovery.

documentation.readme: foremost-ng is a Linux-based file recovery tool that extracts files from disk images or devices by scanning for configurable file headers and footers. It supports common forensic formats (e.g., .dd, Safeback, Encase) and can operate on both image files and live devices. > ⚡ This fork aims to modernize the codebase, improve terminal output with ANSI styling, and introduce new features for forensic analysis and data recovery.
usage_sections: Features: - 🧩 Recover files based on headers and footers. - ⚙️ Configurable file signatures via a plain-text configuration file. - 🪛 Supports header-only or header–footer scans. - 🧱 Works on forensic images or live drives. - 🦠 VirusTotal lookup by file hash (requires a VT API key). ---
Installation: Clone or download the foremost-ng archive, then build and install according to your platform: ```bash
Usage: Run foremost-ng with the appropriate command-line options. For full details, refer to the manual page: Basic syntax: ---

arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.
